questions.question: Who laid the foundation of the Delhi Sultanate in 1206?
questions.explanation: Qutb-ud-din Aibak laid the foundation of the Delhi Sultanate in 1206 after the death of Muhammad Ghori. He was crowned in Lahore and became the first independent Muslim ruler of northern India.

questions.question: The Qutub Minar was named after which Sufi saint?
questions.explanation: The Qutub Minar was named after the Sufi saint Khwaja Qutb-ud-din Bakhtiyar Kaki. Its construction was started by Qutb-ud-din Aibak and completed by Iltutmish.

questions.question: Who is considered the real founder of the Delhi Sultanate?
questions.explanation: Although Aibak laid the foundation, Iltutmish is considered the real founder of the Delhi Sultanate. He shifted the capital from Lahore to Delhi, consolidated the empire, introduced the iqta system, silver tanka and copper jital coins.

questions.question: Which of the following coins was first introduced by Iltutmish?
options: C) Silver tanka and copper jital
questions.correct_answer: C) Silver tanka and copper jital
questions.explanation: Iltutmish was the first Muslim king to introduce the silver tanka and copper jital coins. He was also the first to introduce the 'Pure Arabic Coin' in India.

questions.question: What was the Chahalgani?
options: B) Iltutmish's group of forty faithful slaves
questions.correct_answer: B) Iltutmish's group of forty faithful slaves
questions.explanation: The Chahalgani (also called The Forty) was a group of faithful slaves maintained by Iltutmish. They became very influential in the administration of the Sultanate.

questions.question: How did Qutb-ud-din Aibak die?
questions.explanation: Qutb-ud-din Aibak died in 1210 while playing polo (also called chaugan). He fell from his horse during the game.

questions.question: Which practice did Balban introduce to establish the dignity of the Sultan?
questions.explanation: Balban introduced the practice of Zaminbosi or Sijda, where people had to kneel down and touch the ground before the Sultan. He also propagated the Divine Rights theory.

questions.question: Who was the primary source of information about Raziya Sultan?
questions.explanation: Minhaj-i-Siraj's work is the primary source of information about Raziya Sultan. She was the first and last Muslim woman to rule over Delhi.

questions.question: Who founded the Khalji Dynasty?
questions.explanation: Jalal-ud-din Firuz Khalji founded the Khalji Dynasty in 1290. He was around 70 years old and was a weak and lenient ruler. He was later murdered by his nephew Ala-ud-din Khalji.

questions.question: Ala-ud-din Khalji was the first Sultan to:
options: B) Not ask for manshur from the Caliph
questions.correct_answer: B) Not ask for manshur from the Caliph
questions.explanation: Ala-ud-din Khalji was the first Sultan who did not ask for manshur (letter of authority) from the Caliph. Instead, he called himself the deputy of the Caliph.

questions.question: What were 'Dagh' and 'Huliya' introduced by Ala-ud-din Khalji?
options: B) Branding of horses and descriptive rolls of soldiers
questions.correct_answer: B) Branding of horses and descriptive rolls of soldiers
questions.explanation: Dagh was the system of branding horses and Huliya (or Chehra) was the system of maintaining descriptive rolls of soldiers. These were military reforms introduced by Ala-ud-din Khalji.

questions.question: Which of the following was the best creation of Ala-ud-din Khalji?
questions.explanation: The Alai Darwaza, built near the Qutub Minar, was considered the best creation of Ala-ud-din Khalji. He also built Siri Fort and the Palace of One Thousand Pillars.

questions.question: Who was the court poet of Ala-ud-din Khalji?
questions.explanation: Amir Khusrau was the famous court poet during Ala-ud-din Khalji's reign. Ala-ud-din was a great patron of education, literature, and arts.

questions.question: What was Ala-ud-din Khalji's revenue rate for the fertile Ganga-Yamuna Doab?
questions.explanation: Ala-ud-din Khalji fixed the revenue at one-half of the produce for the fertile Ganga-Yamuna Doab region. Other areas paid one-third. His measures were harsh on peasants.

questions.question: The founder of the Tughlaq Dynasty was:
questions.explanation: Ghazi Malik founded the Tughlaq Dynasty in 1320, taking the title Ghiyas-ud-din Tughlaq. He was half-Indian and half-Turkish.

questions.question: Muhammad-bin-Tughlaq transferred the capital from Delhi to:
questions.explanation: In 1327, Muhammad-bin-Tughlaq transferred the capital from Delhi to Devagiri, which he renamed Daulatabad. The scheme failed and he transferred back to Delhi in 1335.

questions.question: What token currency did Muhammad-bin-Tughlaq introduce?
options: A) Gold mohur in place of silver tanka
B) Copper jittal in place of silver tanka
C) Iron coin in place of gold coin
D) Bronze coin in place of copper coin
questions.correct_answer: B) Copper jittal in place of silver tanka
questions.explanation: Muhammad-bin-Tughlaq introduced copper jittal as token currency in place of silver tanka. People easily forged the copper coins, and he was forced to withdraw the token currency.

questions.question: Firoz Shah Tughlaq's land tax (Kharaj) was fixed at what rate?
questions.explanation: Firoz Shah Tughlaq fixed Kharaj (land tax) at one-tenth (1/10) of the produce. This was much lower than the harsh taxes imposed by earlier rulers like Ala-ud-din Khalji.

questions.question: Which of the following towns was NOT built by Firoz Shah Tughlaq?
questions.explanation: Siri Fort was built by Ala-ud-din Khalji in 1302, not by Firoz Shah Tughlaq. Firoz Shah built Jaunpur, Firozpur, Firozabad, and Hissar-Firoza.

questions.question: Who invaded India in 1398 and left the Sultanate crumbling?
questions.explanation: Timur invaded India in 1398, devastating Delhi and leaving the Sultanate crumbling. By 1412, the Sultanate was confined to a small territory.

questions.question: Who founded the Sayyid Dynasty?
questions.explanation: Khizr Khan founded the Sayyid Dynasty in 1414. He was Timur's viceroy and took the title Rayati-Ala. He was the most competent Sayyid ruler.

questions.question: Which Lodi ruler moved the capital from Delhi to Agra?
questions.explanation: Sikandar Lodi moved the capital from Delhi to Agra to better control the eastern territories. His real name was Nizam Khan and he was the greatest Lodi ruler.

questions.question: Who invited Babur to invade India?
options: C) Daulat Khan Lodi of Punjab
questions.correct_answer: C) Daulat Khan Lodi of Punjab
questions.explanation: Daulat Khan Lodi, Ibrahim Lodi's uncle and governor of Punjab, invited Babur at Kabul to invade India. This led to the Battle of Panipat in 1526 and the end of the Delhi Sultanate.

questions.question: Which department was created by Balban for military affairs?
questions.explanation: Balban created the Diwan-i-Arz for military affairs. This helped him strengthen the army and maintain control over the Sultanate.

questions.question: The Vijayanagara kingdom was founded in which year during the Tughlaq period?
questions.explanation: The Vijayanagara kingdom was founded in 1336, during Muhammad-bin-Tughlaq's reign. The Bahmani kingdom was founded in 1345. Both emerged as revolts against Tughlaq rule weakened.
